Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Net sales increased 8 percent driven by stronger-than-expected iPhone demand in the back-to-school season and continued growth in Services revenue, which reached a new all-time quarterly record, partially offset by a decline in Mac and iPad.

iPhone revenue declined 2 percent year-over-year as competitive intensity increased in China, partially offset by strength in the premium tier and product cycle dynamics, with unit volume compression persisting in emerging market geographies.

Gross margin remained stable sequentially as pricing discipline and a favorable mix shift toward higher-margin Services revenue offset modest increases in manufacturing costs, following the approximately 60 basis point expansion driven by services mix.

We returned over 25 billion dollars to shareholders during the quarter through share repurchases and dividends, consistent with our long-standing capital return program and board authorization.

Apple Intelligence features launched in select markets and languages, contributing to strong upgrade intent among our installed base and supporting the long-term hardware upgrade cycle.

Item 3. Other Information
